narrative.headline: 2018 Honda Civic: steering leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Steering-system behavior is the most-reported concern, with owners describing unexpected vehicle corrections — linked to the Road Departure Mitigation system — that they say created dangerous situations. Fuel-system complaints are also prominent, with owners reporting difficulty starting the vehicle after it has sat unused for several days. A separate thread of fuel-related reports describes a gasoline smell inside the cabin, accompanied by concerns about oil contamination.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report that the Road Departure Mitigation system made an abrupt steering correction that they believe could cause or contribute to a serious accident. · 8 crash

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: Owners report that after the vehicle sat parked and unused for several days, it failed to start upon their return. · 2 crash · 2 injured

issues.blurb: Owners report noticing a fuel smell inside the cabin and, upon checking, finding signs of oil dilution associated with the engine. · 1 crash

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 52 reports (9%), including the only fire in the data, plus 2 crashes and 2 injuries. The fire makes this worth a close look.
